Description: Timer configured but interrupt not firing. Set up in SysCtlPeripheralEnable but handler never called.
Outcome: Found: needed to enable both SysCtlPeripheralEnable AND NVIC_EN0_INT19 (forgot NVIC enable). Fixed clock config too.
Notes: Realized need checklist for timer setup

Description: LCD initialization succeeds but display shows garbage characters. No obvious pattern.
Outcome: Discovered: GPIO pins were set to input mode instead of output for data lines. Also initialization timing needed delays between commands.
Notes: Used oscilloscope to verify timing. Added 1ms delays between LCD commands.

Description: Potentiometer connected to ADC but always reads same value (0xFFF). Voltage measured at pin is correct.
Outcome: Issue was ADC sample time set to 0. Also hardware trigger was enabled. Switched to software trigger and set proper sample rate.
Notes: Check ADC configuration checklist next time - would have caught this faster

Description: Push button works but generates ~5 bounces per press. Interrupt fires 5x instead of once.
Outcome: Added software debounce: GPIO interrupt disabled, software timer waits 20ms, then re-enables interrupt. Tested at 50 bounces/sec.
Notes: Physical filter would also work but debounce is standard practice

Description: Game paddle and ball position logic seems wrong. Ball bounces off paddle at wrong angles.
Outcome: Traced code step-by-step with AI. Found: collision box coordinates were using screen pixels but game math used game units (different scales). Fixed coordinate transformation.
Notes: Needed to draw diagram of coordinate systems to understand. Great learning moment about abstraction layers

Description: After collecting points, game sometimes stops responding. Seems like state machine is stuck.
Outcome: State machine had transition condition that was never true. AI prompted: 'What condition brings you OUT of this state?' Realized loop condition never became false because counter wasn't incrementing.
Notes: This would be good candidate for unit testing each state transition

Description: Timer interrupt fires but timing is off. Countdown is too slow (10s test takes 15s).
Outcome: Clock rate mismatch: TivaC configured for 80MHz but timer math assumed 16MHz. Fixed clock prescaler calculation.
Notes: Created reference sheet: TivaC clock configuration formula. Time to save for future projects

Description: Choosing between PWM-based speed control (Design A) vs. encoder feedback controller (Design B). Which meets <50ms response time requirement?
Outcome: Realized I could start simple with Design A and add feedback later if needed. Learned about real-time vs. throughput tradeoff.
Notes: Modify design doc to include Design A as Phase 1, Design B as Phase 2

Description: Requirement: 'Temperature sensor updates LCD within 200ms'. Test: place sensor in ice water and watch display. Does this test prove the requirement?
Outcome: Need to measure actual timing with oscilloscope. Need to test rapid temp changes, sensor disconnect, LCD error. My test was incomplete.

Description: SPI data transmission successful but garbage characters on LCD. Data format issue suspected.
Outcome: Realized bit ordering was reversed. SPI was sending LSB first but LCD expected MSB first. Fixed configuration.
Notes: Great lesson on reading datasheets carefully for bit ordering
